**Type:** Device Configuration  
**Platform:** Android Enterprise (Android device owner / dedicated device)

### Description
This device-owner configuration targets dedicated (kiosk) Android devices and places the device into single-app kiosk mode using the app de.ozerov.fully (Fully Kiosk Browser). It explicitly blocks Bluetooth, forces the microphone to be muted, and requires Google Play Verify Apps (securityRequireVerifyApps = true). External media and USB file transfer are explicitly allowed (storageBlockExternalMedia = false, storageBlockUsbFileTransfer = false), VPN always-on lockdown is disabled, and password behavior remains at device defaults. Many kiosk customization options (status bar, system navigation, allowed Wi‑Fi SSIDs, etc.) are left not configured, so the primary enforced restrictions are the single-app lock, Bluetooth disablement, microphone mute, and app verification.

## macOS Updates

**Type:** Device Configuration  
**Platform:** macOS

### Description
This macOS software update policy sets criticalUpdateBehavior to "installASAP" so critical fixes are installed as soon as available, and sets allOtherUpdateBehavior to "installLater" with maxUserDeferralsCount = 3 allowing users to defer non‑critical updates up to three times before enforcement. The updateScheduleType is "alwaysUpdate" with priority "low", and there are no custom maintenance windows or UTC offset defined (customUpdateTimeWindows is empty and updateTimeWindowUtcOffsetInMinutes is null), so updates are applied whenever available following a low‑priority cadence. FirmwareUpdateBehavior and ConfigDataUpdateBehavior remain at platform defaults, meaning firmware and configuration data updates follow macOS default handling; overall this reduces risk by immediately applying critical fixes while allowing limited user deferral for other updates.

## MacOS_Default_ActivateSso

**Type:** Device Configuration  
**Platform:** macOS

### Description
Enables the macOS Azure AD Single Sign-On extension to provide seamless sign-on on targeted macOS devices. The policy configures browser_sso_interaction_enabled = 1 to allow the SSO extension to interact with browsers and disable_explicit_app_prompt = 1 to suppress explicit prompts when apps request SSO tokens; shared device mode is disabled (per-user SSO). No bundle ID access control list is defined, so there are no explicit app allow/deny entries configured. All other device feature settings (content caching, power controls, login window text, etc.) remain at their defaults/not configured.

Bitwarden, Inc. is the parent company of 8bit Solutions LLC.

Bitwarden is the easiest and safest way to store all of your logins and passwords while conveniently keeping them synced between all of your devices.

Password theft is a serious problem. The websites and apps that you use are under attack every day. Security breaches occur and your passwords are stolen. When you reuse the same passwords across apps and websites hackers can easily access your email, bank, and other important accounts.

Security experts recommend that you use a different, randomly generated password for every account that you create. But how do you manage all those passwords? Bitwarden makes it easy for you to create, store, and access your passwords.

Bitwarden stores all of your logins in an encrypted vault that syncs across all of your devices. Since it's fully encrypted before it ever leaves your device, only you have access to your data. Not even the team at Bitwarden can read your data, even if we wanted to. Your data is sealed with AES-256 bit encryption, salted hashing, and PBKDF2 SHA-256.

Bitwarden is 100% open source software. The source code for Bitwarden is hosted on GitHub and everyone is free to review, audit, and contribute to the Bitwarden codebase.
